Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First, lets look at the activities and their sequences per case: Case 1: OR -> MS -> QS -> AS -> IQC -> AS -> TST -> RT -> TST -> PK -> LB -> DP -> D Case 2: OR -> MS -> QS -> AS -> IQC -> AS -> TST -> PK -> DP -> D Case 3: OR -> MS -> QS -> AS -> IQC -> IQC -> AS -> IQC -> AS -> TST -> RT -> TST -> PK -> LB -> DP -> D Case 4: OR -> MS -> QS -> AS -> TST -> PK -> LB -> DP -> D Case 5: OR -> MS -> QS -> AS -> IQC -> AS -> IQC -> AS -> TST -> RT -> TST -> RT -> TST -> PK -> LB -> DP -> D

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Let me identify patterns: 1. OR always happens first. 2. MS always follows OR. 3. QS always follows MS. 4. AS always follows QS. Then theres an optional/repeated pattern involving IQC and AS

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1: AS -> IQC -> AS Case 2: AS -> IQC -> AS Case 3: AS -> IQC -> IQC -> AS -> IQC -> AS Case 4: AS -> TST Case 5: AS -> IQC -> AS -> IQC -> AS

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: the pattern is that AS and IQC form a loop: do AS, then IQC, and if it fails, do AS again, then IQC again, etc. If A = AS and B = IQC, then: do AS, then either exit or do IQC then AS again. This matches cases 1, 2, 5.

Name: Validation - IND
Text: But case 3 has AS->IQC->IQC->AS. Thats unusual. However, given the problem description, I think *(AS, IQC) is the intended model

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Cases show: Case 1: TST -> RT -> TST Case 2: TST Case 3: TST -> RT -> TST Case 4: TST Case 5: TST -> RT -> TST -> RT -> TST This is clearly a loop: do TST, then optionally do RT and then TST again. So *(TST, RT).

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: LB: Case 1: PK -> LB -> DP Case 2: PK -> DP Case 3: PK -> LB -> DP Case 4: PK -> LB -> DP Case 5: PK -> LB -> DP So LB is optional. This is a choice: X(LB, skip)

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: the overall structure: OR -> MS -> QS -> Loop1 -> Loop2 -> PK -> Choice -> DP -> D Then the root partial order: nodes = [OR, MS, QS, Loop1, Loop2, PK, Xor, DP, D] edges: OR -> MS MS -> QS QS -> Loop1 Loop1 -> Loop2 Loop2 -> PK PK -> Xor Xor -> DP DP -> D

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This gives traces: AS, or AS-IQC-AS, or AS-IQC-AS-IQC-AS, etc. This matches. For the testing loop: TST, or TST-RT-TST, or TST-RT-TST-RT-TST, etc. This matches all cases perfectly.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: So the model seems solid. The partial order enforces a pipeline where each stage completes before the next begins
